questions.text: If A = 2i + 3j - k , B = 4i + 2j - 2k . Find a vector x which is parallel to A but has the magnitude of B?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option correctly scales vector A to have the magnitude of vector B while maintaining its direction, making it parallel to A.
This option is simply vector A, which has the correct direction but not the required magnitude equal to vector B.
This is vector B itself, which has the correct magnitude but not the direction parallel to vector A.
This option incorrectly scales vector A, resulting in a vector with the incorrect magnitude.
This vector neither has the correct direction nor the correct magnitude to be parallel to A with B's magnitude.

questions.explanation: The magnitude of B is equal to √(4)² + (2)² + (2)²=√24The magnitude of A is equal to √(2)² + (3)² + (1)² =√14The parallel vector is proportional to vector A.=>√24/√14 (2i + 3j - k)=>√(12/7) (2i + 3j - k)

questions.text: A vector such as the velocity of a body undergoing uniform translational motion, which can be displaced parallel to itself and applied to any point known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A vector whose magnitude is one is called a unit vector.

A free vector is one whose action is not confined or associated with a unique line in space. For a body in uniform translational motion, the velocity of any point in the body may be taken as a vector and this vector will equally well describe the velocity of all points in the body.
A directionless vector whose magnitude is zero is called a null vector.
A position vector is defined as a vector that symbolizes either the position or the location of any given point concerning any arbitrary reference point.
A resultant vector is defined as a single vector that produces the same effect as is produced by a number of vectors collectively.

questions.explanation: Option B is the correct answer because a free vector is one whose action is not confined or associated with a unique line in space. For a body in uniform translational motion, the velocity of any point in the body may be taken as a vector and this vector will equally well describe the velocity of all points in the body. Hence, the velocity of such a body may be represented by a free vector.

questions.text: R1 and R2 are two-position vectors making angles θ1 and θ2 with positive X-axis respectively. Their vector product is:[R1 = 4 cm, R2 = 3 cm , θ1 = 30 degrees , θ2 = 90 ]
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. It results from a miscalculation of the sine component or swapping of angles.
This is the correct answer. The vector product is calculated using the formula |R1 × R2| = |R1||R2|sin(θ2 - θ1). Substituting the given values, we find |R1 × R2| = 6√3 cm, directed along the Z-axis.
This option is incorrect due to an error in simplifying the trigonometric component or incorrect angle substitution.
This option is incorrect. It likely stems from an incorrect use of the trigonometric function or angle values.
This option is incorrect and results from either a calculation error in the sine component or incorrect angle usage.

questions.explanation: The vector product of two vectors, R1 and R2, is given by the formula |R1 × R2| = |R1| |R2| sin(θ2 - θ1), where θ is the angle between the vectors. Here, |R1| = 4 cm, |R2| = 3 cm, θ1 = 30°, and θ2 = 90°. Thus, |R1 × R2| = 4 × 3 × sin(90° - 30°) = 4 × 3 × 1/2√3 = 6√3 cm.This calculation confirms that the correct answer is 6√3 cm, directed along the Z-axis.The incorrect options result from errors in either the trigonometric calculations or angle substitutions, leading to incorrect values.

questions.text: If A = 3i + 6j - 2k, then the unit vector parallel to A will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it does not correctly calculate the magnitude of vector A.
This option is correct as it divides each component of vector A by its magnitude, 7, resulting in the unit vector.
This option incorrectly uses 14 as the magnitude, which is incorrect for vector A.
This option represents vector A itself, not the unit vector parallel to A.
This option multiplies by the magnitude instead of dividing, which is incorrect for finding a unit vector.

questions.explanation: To find a unit vector parallel to a given vector A = 3i + 6j - 2k, first calculate the magnitude of A: |A| = √(3² + 6² + (-2)²) = √(9 + 36 + 4) = √49 = 7. The unit vector, denoted as û, is then calculated by dividing each component of A by its magnitude: û = (1/7)(3i + 6j - 2k) = (3/7)i + (6/7)j - (2/7)k. Therefore, the correct answer is (1/7) * (3i + 6j - 2k). The other options either use incorrect magnitudes or represent the vector A itself, not the unit vector.

questions.text: The scalar product of (2i - j + 3k).(3i + 2j - k)?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is correct. Calculating the scalar product: (2i-j+3k) • (3i+2j-k) = 2×3 + (-1)×2 + 3×(-1) = 6 - 2 - 3 = 1.
This is incorrect. Check the signs and operations used in calculating the scalar product.
This is incorrect. You may have miscalculated the component products or their sum.
This is incorrect. This result is too high; re-evaluate the multiplication of the components.
This is incorrect. This value is not consistent with the correct calculation of the scalar product.

questions.explanation: The scalar product (also known as the dot product) of two vectors is calculated by multiplying their corresponding components and adding the results: (2i-j+3k) • (3i+2j-k) = 2×3 + (-1)×2 + 3×(-1) = 6 - 2 - 3 = 1. Options B, C, D, and E are incorrect as they result from miscalculations in either the component multiplication or the addition of these products.

questions.text: The resultant of two forces has magnitude of 20N. If one of the forces is of magnitude 20√3 N and makes an angle of 30o with the resultant then, the other forces must be of magnitude:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Please refer to the main explanation text.

To calculate the magnitude of the other force, we can use trigonometry and vector addition. The given force is 20√3 N at an angle of 30 degrees. Let's break down the force into x and y components:The x-component of the force = 20√3 * cos(30°) = 20NThe y-component of the force = 20√3 * sin(30°) = 10NSince the resultant force is 20N, and one force is already 20N, the other force should balance out the y-component, which is 10N. Therefore, the magnitude of the other force must also be 20N to cancel out the y-component and give a resultant of 20N.
Please refer to the main explanation text.
Please refer to the main explanation text.
Please refer to the main explanation text.

questions.explanation: Correct option is 'B'.

questions.text: A vector in space has _ dimension:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A vector in space has three dimensions, x, y and z.
A vector in space has three dimensions, x, y and z.
A vector in space has three dimensions, x, y and z.
A vector in space has three dimensions, x, y and z.

questions.explanation: The "three" dimensions are x, y, and z. Three-dimensional space (also: 3D space, 3-space or, rarely, tri-dimensional space) is a geometric setting in which three values (called parameters) are required to determine the position of an element (i.e., point). This is the informal meaning of the term dimension.

questions.text: The vector product of two vectors A and B is _ vectors A and B.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Not equal to the product of magnitudes of
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The vector product, or cross product, results in a vector, not a scalar, and its magnitude is given by |A||B|sin(θ), not just the product of magnitudes.
This option is incorrect. The cross product results in a vector that is perpendicular to, not parallel to, the plane containing the original vectors.
Correct! The cross product of two vectors A and B results in a vector that is perpendicular to the plane containing A and B, as determined by the right-hand rule.

options.text: Less in magnitude than the product of magnitudes of
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The magnitude of the cross product is based on |A||B|sin(θ), and it can be less than, equal to, or greater than the simple product of magnitudes, depending on the angle between the vectors.

questions.explanation: The vector product, or cross product, of two vectors A and B results in a vector perpendicular to the plane containing A and B. This is determined using the right-hand rule, which helps visualize the direction of the resulting vector. The magnitude of the cross product is |A × B| = |A||B|sin(θ), where θ is the angle between A and B. Option C correctly identifies the perpendicular nature of the resulting vector. Option A is incorrect because it misrepresents the nature of the cross product as a scalar. Option B is incorrect because it misinterprets the orientation as parallel rather than perpendicular. Option D is incorrect because it inaccurately describes the magnitude relationship without considering the sine component.

questions.text: A constant force F= 2i + 3j +4k is applied on a body. What will be the work done to move the body 5m in the z-direction?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect because there is a component of force in the z-direction, which means work is done.
This is incorrect because the calculation of work done involves multiplying the force component in the z-direction by the displacement, which does not yield 10 J.
This is incorrect because it assumes the entire magnitude of the force is applied in the z-direction, which is not the case.
This is correct. The work done is calculated by multiplying the force component in the z-direction (4 N) by the displacement (5 m), resulting in 20 J.

questions.explanation: We know that work done = F x dwhere F is the force and d is the displacement in the direction of the force.The question statement says a constant force, F = 2i + 3j + 4k is applied. The component of force in the z-direction (direction of displacement of the body) = 4NTherefore, Work done = 4 x 5This equals to 20 J.

questions.text: Displacement of sun with respect to earth is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. At any given instant, the displacement of the Sun with respect to Earth is approximately r, which is the radius of Earth's nearly circular orbit. Displacement is the shortest path between two points.
Incorrect. 2πr is the circumference of the Earth's orbit, representing the total path length of one full revolution, not the displacement at any specific moment.

Incorrect. 2r might represent the displacement if the Earth moved directly across its orbit from one side to the opposite side, but this is not generally the case for displacement at any given moment.
Incorrect. r² represents the square of the radius, which is not related to displacement. Displacement is a vector quantity that involves distance and direction, not squared values.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is r. Displacement is a vector quantity that describes the shortest path between two points, taking into account direction. At any given moment, the displacement of the Sun with respect to the Earth is approximately the radius of Earth's nearly circular orbit, r. The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately describe displacement: 2πr is the path length of a full orbit, 2r is only applicable in a specific situation not generally true at any moment, and r² is unrelated to displacement.

questions.text: If the angle between two forces increases, the magnitude of their resultant:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. As the angle between two vectors increases, the magnitude of their resultant decreases according to the vector addition formula.
Incorrect. An increase in angle between vectors leads to a smaller resultant magnitude, not a larger one.
Incorrect. The resultant magnitude changes with the angle; it does not remain constant.
Incorrect. The resultant magnitude consistently decreases as the angle increases, up to 180 degrees.

questions.explanation: The magnitude of the resultant force in vector addition is given by the formula: R = √(A² + B² + 2ABcosθ), where A and B are the magnitudes of the two forces and θ is the angle between them. As the angle θ increases from 0 to 180 degrees, the cosine of the angle decreases from 1 to -1, thus reducing the value of the resultant force R. Therefore, the magnitude of the resultant decreases as the angle increases.

questions.text: The greatest and smallest resultant of two forces acting at a point is 10 N and 6 N respectively. If each force is increased by 3 N, find the magnitude of the new resultant forces when acting at a point while being perpendicular to each other:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The resultant force is calculated using the Pythagorean theorem, which was not applied correctly here.
This is the correct answer. When two forces are acting at right angles, the resultant is found using the Pythagorean theorem: R = √(F1² + F2²).
This option is incorrect. This value doesn't result from the correct application of the Pythagorean theorem to the increased forces.
This option is incorrect. The calculation here doesn't correctly apply the formula for the resultant of perpendicular forces.

questions.explanation: To solve the problem, recognize that the greatest resultant (10 N) and smallest resultant (6 N) of two forces F1 and F2 are given by the formulas: F1 + F2 = 10 and |F1 - F2| = 6. Solving these equations gives F1 = 8 N and F2 = 2 N. When each force is increased by 3 N, the new forces become F1 = 11 N and F2 = 5 N. Since the forces are perpendicular, use the formula for the resultant of perpendicular vectors: R = √(F1² + F2²) = √(11² + 5²) = √(121 + 25) = √146 ≈ 12.1 N. Thus, the correct answer is 12.1 N. The other options do not correctly apply this calculation method.

questions.text: Addition, subtraction and multiplication of scalars is done by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Scalars are quantities that only have magnitude, and their operations do not involve directions. Therefore, they follow algebraic principles like addition, subtraction, and multiplication.

Addition, subtraction, and multiplication of scalars are done using the ordinary arithmetic rules of real numbers. Scalars are simply single numbers (not vectors or matrices), so the operations are straightforward:Addition of scalars:a+bwhere a and b are scalars.Subtraction of scalars:a−bMultiplication of scalars:a×b or ab
Logical methods pertain more to reasoning processes and logic operations, not mathematical operations with scalars.
Vector algebra involves operations on quantities with both magnitude and direction, which is not applicable to scalars.

questions.explanation: Addition, subtraction, and multiplication of scalars are done using the ordinary arithmetic rules of real numbers. Scalars are simply single numbers (not vectors or matrices), so the operations are straightforward:Addition of scalars:a+bwhere a and b are scalars.Subtraction of scalars:a−bMultiplication of scalars:a×b or ab

questions.text: A unit vector is obtained by dividing the given vector by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A unit vector is obtained by dividing the given vector by its magnitude or length. This process is done to normalize the vector, resulting in a vector that has a magnitude of 1 while retaining the direction of the original vector.

Dividing a vector by its angle doesn't yield a meaningful mathematical operation. Instead, to obtain a unit vector, you divide the vector by its magnitude (or length), not by its angle. The angle between vectors is used in various calculations, such as determining components or projections, but division of a vector by an angle isn't a standard mathematical operation.

Dividing one vector by another vector isn't directly defined in standard vector algebra. There is a concept known as the 'vector quotient,' but it's not a typical arithmetic operation like addition or multiplication. It's often used in advanced mathematics or physics contexts, where the division of vectors might be represented in terms of the cross product and the inverse of a vector. However, straightforward division between two vectors in the way one might divide numbers is not a standard operation in vector mathematics.
This option is incorrect. It does not give us a unit vector.

questions.explanation: A unit vector is obtained by dividing the given vector by its magnitude or length. This process is done to normalize the vector, resulting in a vector that has a magnitude of 1 while retaining the direction of the original vector.Dividing by the angle, another vector, or a numerical value like ten will not result in a unit vector as defined in vector algebra. It is essential to focus on the magnitude of the vector when seeking a unit vector.

questions.text: The vector obtained by adding two or more vectors is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The vector obtained by adding two or more vectors is called the resultant vector, not a product vector.
This option is incorrect. The vector obtained by adding two or more vectors is called the resultant vector, not a sum vector.

When you add two or more vectors together, you're combining their effects to find a single vector that represents the overall effect or sum of those individual vectors. This resulting vector is referred to as the 'resultant vector.' It encapsulates the combined impact of all the initial vectors in terms of both magnitude and direction.
This option is incorrect. The vector obtained by adding two or more vectors is called the resultant vector, not a final vector.

questions.explanation: When you add two or more vectors together, you're combining their effects to find a single vector that represents the overall effect or sum of those individual vectors. This resulting vector is referred to as the 'resultant vector.' It encapsulates the combined impact of all the initial vectors in terms of both magnitude and direction.

questions.text: Vectors are added according to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Left hand rule is used in electromagnetics.
This option is incorrect. Right hand rule is used in electromagnetics.
This is the correct option. When adding vectors, the graphical method involves placing the head of one vector at the tail of the previous one to determine the resultant vector.
As one of the above options is correct, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: When adding vectors, the head to tail rule is used in the graphical method of vector addition. This rule helps determine the direction and magnitude of the resultant vector by connecting the head of one vector to the tail of the previous one. The other options, left hand rule and right hand rule, are not applicable to vector addition and are used in electromagnetics for different purposes.

questions.text: The resultant of two or more vectors is obtained by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Joining the tail of the first vector with the head of the last vector
options.explanation: This option is correct. The head-to-tail method involves connecting vectors in sequence to determine the resultant vector accurately.

options.text: Joining the head of the first vector with the tail of the last vector
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. It does not follow the head-to-tail method for vector addition.

options.text: Joining the tail of the last vector with the head of the first vector
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. It reverses the order of vector connection, which is not in line with the head-to-tail method.

options.text: Joining the head of the last vector with the tail of the first vector
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. It does not adhere to the correct procedure of connecting vectors in the head-to-tail fashion.

questions.explanation: The head-to-tail method is used to find the resultant of vectors by sequentially connecting them so that the head of one vector is joined to the tail of the next. The resultant vector is then drawn from the tail of the first vector to the head of the last vector. This method accurately determines the magnitude and direction of the resultant vector. The other options provide incorrect ways of connecting vectors that do not align with the head-to-tail rule, leading to inaccurate results.

questions.text: If the x-component of the resultant of two vectors is positive and its y-component is negative, the resultant subtends an angle of _ on x-axes.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the x-component is positive and the y-component is negative, the vector lies in the fourth quadrant. The angle it makes with the positive x-axis is given by 360° - θ. Therefore, this option is correct.
This option implies the vector lies in the third quadrant, which is incorrect given the x-component is positive and the y-component is negative. Thus, it cannot be correct.
This suggests the vector lies between the first and second quadrants, implying a positive y-component, which contradicts the given condition of a negative y-component.
This implies the vector lies in the first quadrant, which is incorrect as the y-component is negative, placing the vector in the fourth quadrant.

questions.explanation: To determine the angle subtended by the resultant vector on the x-axis, consider the signs of its components. A positive x-component and a negative y-component place the vector in the fourth quadrant. In this quadrant, the angle is measured as 360° - θ relative to the positive x-axis. Therefore, the correct answer is 360° - θ. Other options suggest placement of the vector in incorrect quadrants based on their angle indications, which do not match the given component signs.

questions.text: The vector quantities are described by their magnitude as well as _

options.explanation: Distance is a scalar quantity and does not involve direction, making it an incorrect option.
Vector quantities are described by their magnitude as well as direction. The direction indicates the orientation or the way in which the vector points in space.
Speed is a scalar quantity that only represents the rate of motion without considering direction, making it an incorrect option.
Acceleration is a vector quantity, but it describes the rate of change of velocity rather than the direction of the vector, making it an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Vector quantities require both magnitude and direction to be fully described. This is because the direction indicates the orientation or the way in which the vector points in space. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Direction'. The other options, such as Distance, Speed, and Acceleration, do not encompass the crucial aspect of direction required for vector quantities.

questions.text: Velocity is a_quantity.

options.explanation: Velocity is displacement per unit time and has a specific direction. It contains direction as well as magnitude of a moving object, so velocity is a vector quantity.
Speed is a scalar quantity that only represents the magnitude of velocity without considering direction.
A scalar quantity only has magnitude and no specified direction, unlike velocity which includes both magnitude and direction.
This option does not correctly describe the nature of velocity as a vector quantity with direction and magnitude.

questions.explanation: Velocity is a vector quantity because it includes both magnitude (speed) and direction. The other options are incorrect because they either describe scalar quantities or do not address the vector nature of velocity.

questions.text: Along the three mutually perpendicular axes x, y and z, the unit vectors are denoted by:

questions.text: In negative of a vector, a vector has same magnitude but _ direction.

Therefore, in the negative of a vector, the direction is opposite while the magnitude remains unchanged.

questions.text: Let we have a vector F, then its horizontal component is written as:

This option confuses the horizontal and vertical components. FY refers to the vertical component, which should use sine, not cosine.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Fx = |F|cosθ. This formula defines the horizontal component of a vector as the magnitude of the vector times the cosine of the angle between the vector and the positive x-axis. Using sinθ, tanθ, or secθ in place of cosθ would result in incorrect calculations for the horizontal component.

questions.text: If the x-component of the resultant is negative and its y-component is positive, the result is true for:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: An angle of (180°- θ) with x-axis
options.explanation: When the x-component is negative and the y-component is positive, the vector is in the second quadrant of the Cartesian coordinate system, making an angle of (180°- θ) with the x-axis.

options.text: An angle of (180°- θ) with y-axis
options.explanation: This option does not correspond to the scenario described in the question where the x-component is negative and the y-component is positive.
An angle of 90° does not align with the situation of a negative x-component and a positive y-component in the second quadrant.
An angle of 180° does not fit the conditions specified in the question regarding the signs of the x and y components.

questions.explanation: When the x-component of the resultant vector is negative and the y-component is positive, the vector is positioned in the second quadrant of the Cartesian coordinate system. In this quadrant, the x-coordinate is negative, and the y-coordinate is positive, leading to an angle of (180°- θ) with the x-axis.The other options are incorrect because they do not align with the specific conditions provided in the question, such as the signs of the x and y components and the quadrant in which the vector lies.

questions.text: When the multiplication of two vectors result into a vector quantity, then the product is called _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The cross product is also known as the vector product, resulting in a vector that is perpendicular to the two vectors being multiplied.
The dot product yields a scalar quantity, not a vector, when two vectors are multiplied together.
Calculating the magnitude of vectors involves using the Pythagorean theorem, it is not the result of multiplying two vectors together.
Since the product of two vectors results in a vector quantity in this case, this option does not apply.

questions.explanation: The cross product, also known as the vector product, results in a vector that is perpendicular to the two vectors being multiplied. It is denoted by a × b and can be calculated using the Right-hand Rule. The dot product of two vectors is a scalar quantity, not a vector, while the magnitude of vectors is determined using the Pythagorean theorem. Therefore, the correct answer is the Cross product.

questions.text: The tangent of an angle is positive in first and _ quadrant.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. The tangent of an angle is positive in the first and third quadrants.
Correct! The tangent of an angle is positive in the first and third quadrants.
Incorrect. The tangent of an angle is positive in the first and third quadrants, not the second quadrant.
Incorrect. The tangent of an angle is positive in the first and third quadrants, not the fifth quadrant.

questions.explanation: The tangent of an angle is positive in the first quadrant and the third quadrant. In the first quadrant, both the sine and cosine are positive, so the tangent which is the ratio of sine to cosine, is positive. In the third quadrant, the sine is negative, the cosine is negative, but their ratio (tangent) is positive.

questions.text: The vector product of two vector L and M can be determined by the formula _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect because this formula represents the dot product, not the vector product.
Correct! The vector product of two vectors L and M is determined by this formula.
Incorrect because the cross product involves the sine of the angle, not the cosine.
Incorrect as one of the options provided is the correct formula for the vector product.

questions.explanation: The correct formula for the vector product of two vectors L and M is L. M Sinθ nˆ. This formula takes into account the magnitudes of the vectors, the sine of the angle between them, and the unit vector in the direction perpendicular to the plane formed by L and M. The other options provided either involve the dot product or incorrect variations of the vector product formula.

questions.text: If the unit vectors i, j, k are perpendicular to each other therefore;i.i = j.j = k.k = _.I.j = j.k = k.i = _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the dot product of a unit vector with itself is always 1, not 0.
This is the correct answer because the dot product of a unit vector with itself (e.g., i.i) is 1. The dot product of two different perpendicular unit vectors (e.g., i.j) is 0.
This option is incorrect because the dot product of a unit vector with itself is not 0; it is 1.
This option is incorrect because the dot product of two different perpendicular unit vectors is 0, not 1.

questions.explanation: The dot product of two vectors A and B is given by A.B = |A||B| cosθ. If the angle θ between the vectors is 0°, cosθ = 1, so a vector dotted with itself (i.i, j.j, k.k) is 1, due to the unit magnitude of the vectors. If θ is 90°, cosθ = 0, so the dot product of two perpendicular vectors (i.j, j.k, k.i) is 0. Therefore, the correct answer is 1 for i.i = j.j = k.k, and 0 for i.j = j.k = k.i. The other options are incorrect as they do not accurately reflect these properties of the dot product for unit vectors.

questions.text: Vector is quantity that ------.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: While it is true that a vector has a direction, this option is incomplete as it does not account for the magnitude that is also essential to define a vector. Therefore, this option alone is not sufficient to describe a vector.
This option highlights that a vector possesses magnitude; however, it fails to mention that direction is equally important. Therefore, this option does not fully define a vector.

This option correctly identifies that vectors must adhere to the rules of vector addition, which include properties such as commutativity and associativity. These rules are vital in understanding how vectors interact and combine, making this option a strong candidate for the correct answer.

This option encompasses all the characteristics of a vector—having direction, having magnitude, and following rules of vector addition. Since a vector indeed possesses all these qualities, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: A vector is defined as a quantity that has both magnitude and direction. In addition to these fundamental properties, vectors also follow specific rules for addition that allow them to be combined in a consistent manner. Option A is incomplete because it only mentions direction. Option B is similarly inadequate, as it only refers to magnitude. Option C, while accurate in its description of vector addition, does not fully capture the essence of what a vector is, as it neglects to mention magnitude and direction. Therefore, the correct answer is Option D, which states that a vector possesses all these properties.

questions.text: If i, j, and k are unit vectors along x, y, and z-axis, then k x j =?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The correct answer is -i. By using the right-hand rule for the cross product of k (z-axis) and j (y-axis), your thumb points in the negative x-direction, which corresponds to -i.
This is not the correct answer. The cross product k x j does not point along the y-axis or its negative direction; it points along the negative x-axis, which is -i.
This is not the correct answer. The result of the cross product k x j is not along the z-axis or its negative, but rather along the negative x-axis, which is -i.
Incorrect. The cross product k x j results in -i, which is a valid option, so 'None of these' is not the correct choice.

questions.explanation: When calculating the cross product (k x j) of the unit vectors along the z-axis (k) and the y-axis (j) using the right-hand rule, the result will be in the negative x-axis direction, corresponding to -i. Therefore, the correct answer is -i.The other options are incorrect because the cross product of k x j does not result in the y-axis unit vector j, the negative y-axis unit vector -j, or the positive x-axis unit vector i.

questions.text: A scalar is a physical quantity which is completely specified by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A scalar is not defined by direction, but by another characteristic.
Correct! A scalar is completely specified by its magnitude alone.
This describes vectors, not scalars.
While scalars do not consider direction, they are defined by magnitude.

questions.explanation: A scalar is a physical quantity that is completely specified by its magnitude alone. Scalars do not have direction, and they are characterized solely by a numerical value and a unit of measurement. Therefore, the correct answer is that a scalar is specified by magnitude only. The other options are incorrect because they either mention direction (which does not apply to scalars) or suggest that both magnitude and direction define a scalar, which is inaccurate.

questions.text: Which of the following is a scalar quantity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Density is a scalar quantity because it only has magnitude and no direction. It is represented by ρ. Since density is derived from all scalar measurements, it remains a scalar measurement.
Displacement is a vector quantity as it possesses both magnitude and direction. It is represented as a change in position from one point to another.
Torque is a vector quantity because its direction depends on the force applied on the axis. It involves rotational motion and has both magnitude and direction.
Weight is a vector quantity as it is a force that has both magnitude and direction. It is the force exerted on an object due to gravity.

questions.explanation: Density is the correct answer as it is a scalar quantity with only magnitude and no direction. Displacement, torque, and weight are all vector quantities as they possess both magnitude and direction, making them unsuitable for scalar classification.

questions.text: Find the Error.A couple of my friends(A) has planned(B) to open(C) a travel agency, but they(D) have failed.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This part of the sentence is correct. 'A couple of my friends' suggests multiple individuals, necessitating a plural verb in the following clause.
This part is incorrect. The phrase 'A couple of my friends' is treated as plural, so it should use 'have planned' instead of 'has planned.'
This part of the sentence is correct. 'To open' functions as an infinitive phrase, appropriately following the verb.
This part of the sentence is correct. The pronoun 'they' correctly refers back to 'friends,' maintaining the plural agreement.

questions.explanation: The phrase 'A couple of my friends' is considered plural, which means the verb that follows should also be plural. Thus, 'have planned' is the correct form to use instead of 'has planned.' The other options are accurate in their grammatical structure: 'A couple of my friends' correctly indicates multiple individuals, 'to open' is an appropriate infinitive phrase following the verb, and 'but they' correctly refers back to the plural noun 'friends.' Therefore, the error lies specifically in the verb form used in option B.

questions.text: Choose the correct option.
questions.subject: English

options.text: We are all really good friends so we would just having a laugh and egging one another on.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because "would just having" is grammatically incorrect. The correct form should be "were just having." Also, "one another" should be "each other" for clarity.

options.text: We are all really good friends so we were just having a laughter and egging each other on.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because "laughter" should be "a laugh" (singular) to match the idiom "having a laugh." Also, "each other" should not have "a" before it.

options.text: We are all really good friends so we were just having a laugh and egging each others on.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because "each others" should be "each other" (without the possessive form) to correctly refer to multiple people egging each other on.

options.text: We are all really good friends so we were just having a laugh and egging each other on.
options.explanation: The correct option is: We are all really good friends so we were just having a laugh and egging each other on.

questions.explanation: The correct option is: We are all really good friends so we were just having a laugh and egging each other on.

questions.text: Select the most suitable synonym for the given word:BECKONED
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: A) Repulse:- Meaning: To drive back or away; to repel.

B) Summoned:- Meaning: To call someone to come or attend."Beckoned" means to signal or call someone to come or approach, which is similar to "summoned". The other options have opposite or unrelated meanings.
C) Surfeit:- Meaning: To satisfy to excess; to overindulge.
D) Dismissed:- Meaning: To let go or send away; to discharge.

questions.explanation: To beckon is to motion or gesture someone to approach you. summon is to order someone to be present.

questions.text: Statements: Video libraries are flourishing very much these days.Conclusions:I. People, in general, have got a video craze.II. It is much cheaper to see as many movies as one likes on videos rather than going to the cinema hall.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I suggests that the popularity of video libraries is due to a general craze for videos, which could be inferred from the flourishing state of video libraries.However, this doesn't rule out other reasons for their popularity.

Conclusion II provides a specific reason for the flourishing of video libraries, stating that it is cheaper to watch videos than going to the cinema, which could attract more people.However, this does not negate the possibility of a video craze also contributing to their popularity.
Either conclusion could independently provide a reason for the flourishing of video libraries, but the statement does not restrict the situation to only one possible explanation.

Conclusion I reflects a broad public interest in videos, aligning with the statement that video libraries are flourishing.Conclusion II offers a plausible economic reason for this trend, suggesting that the cost-effectiveness of videos compared to cinema visits might encourage more people to use video libraries.Both conclusions together provide a comprehensive explanation for the flourishing of video libraries.

questions.explanation: The flourishing of video libraries suggests a high demand, which can be described as a "video craze" (Conclusion I). A plausible reason for this high demand is the costeffectiveness compared to cinemas (Conclusion II). Both conclusions serve as likely causes for the stated fact.

questions.text: In the following question, correct the equation by interchanging two signs.9 x 3 + 8 ÷ 4 - 7 = 28
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Incorrect. Interchanging 'x' and '-' does not satisfy the equation.Expression becomes: 9 - 3 + 8 ÷ 4 x 7Calculate: 9 - 3 + 2 x 7 = 6 + 14 = 20, which is not equal to 28.
Incorrect. Interchanging '+' and '-' does not satisfy the equation.Expression becomes: 9 x 3 - 8 ÷ 4 + 7Calculate: 27 - 2 + 7 = 32, which is not equal to 28.
Incorrect. Interchanging '÷' and '+' does not satisfy the equation.Expression becomes: 9 x 3 ÷ 8 + 4 - 7Calculate: 3.375 + 4 - 7 = 0.375, which is not equal to 28.
Correct. Interchanging 'x' and '÷' satisfies the equation.Expression becomes: 9 ÷ 3 + 8 x 4 - 7Calculate: 3 + 32 - 7 = 28, which matches the given equation.
Incorrect. The correct solution is found by interchanging 'x' and '÷'.

questions.explanation: To solve the problem, we need to interchange two mathematical signs to make the equation correct. Testing each option:Option A: Interchanging 'x' and '-' results in: 9 - 3 + 8 ÷ 4 x 7 = 20, which is not 28.Option B: Interchanging '+' and '-' results in: 9 x 3 - 8 ÷ 4 + 7 = 32, which is not 28.Option C: Interchanging '÷' and '+' results in: 9 x 3 ÷ 8 + 4 - 7 = 0.375, which is not 28.Option D: Interchanging 'x' and '÷' results in: 9 ÷ 3 + 8 x 4 - 7 = 28, which matches the equation.Thus, the correct answer is Option D, where interchanging 'x' and '÷' makes the equation valid.

questions.text: Statements: Irregularity is a cause for failure in exams. Some regular students fail in the examinations.Conclusions:I. All failed students are regular.II. All successful students are not regular.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The first conclusion states that all failed students are regular, which contradicts the statement that irregularity is a cause for failure. Therefore, conclusion I does not logically follow.

The second conclusion suggests that all successful students are not regular. However, the statement only indicates that irregularity is a cause of failure, not success. Hence, conclusion II does not logically follow.
Both conclusions are mutually exclusive and do not logically follow from the given statements. Thus, neither one nor the other can be considered correct.

Given the statements, irregularity is a cause for failure, but some regular students also fail. This suggests that neither all failed students are regular, nor are all successful students irregular. Hence, neither conclusion I nor II logically follows.

The given statements do not support the idea that all failed students are regular or that all successful students are irregular. Therefore, both conclusions cannot be true based on the information provided.

questions.explanation: In evaluating the conclusions, we must carefully consider the information given. The statement 'Irregularity is a cause for failure in exams' implies that irregular students can fail, but it does not rule out the possibility of regular students failing. Thus, conclusion I is incorrect because not all failed students are regular; some could be irregular. Similarly, conclusion II suggests that all successful students are not regular, which isn't supported by the statement. It merely states irregularity leads to failure, not success. Therefore, the correct answer is that neither conclusion I nor II follows logically from the given statements.

questions.text: In the following question, by using which mathematical operators will the expression become correct?18 ? 6 ? 9 ? 27
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This is not correct. By placing x, ÷ and =, we get 18 x 6 ÷ 9 = 27. Simplifying gives 12 = 27, which is incorrect.
This is correct. By placing ÷, x and =, we get 18 ÷ 6 x 9 = 27. Simplifying gives 3 x 9 = 27, which results in 27 = 27.
This is not correct. By placing x, + and =, we get 18 x 6 + 9 = 27. Simplifying gives 117 = 27, which is incorrect.
This is not correct. By placing +, - and =, we get 18 + 6 - 9 = 27. Simplifying gives 15 = 27, which is incorrect.

questions.explanation: By inserting the operators '÷', '×', and '=', the expression becomes 18 ÷ 6 × 9 = 27. This simplifies to 3 × 9 = 27, which is a correct statement.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect; B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect; C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes; D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes; E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes; I. Today's life of IT professionals is hectic, fast, demanding and result oriented so, it leads to a stressful situation. II. Whether it is IT professionals or in any other profession, heart disease is increasing day by day.
